description_long: This is a rectangular stone block with distinct, horizontally layered striations in varying shades of gray, brown, and tan, suggesting sedimentary rock such as sandstone or shale. The block has a rough, uneven surface with visible textural variations and some minor chips and irregularities along the edges. Its top and bottom faces are relatively flat, while the sides display the most pronounced banding and layering. The coloration is natural, with darker bands interspersed with lighter ones, giving it a weathered, aged appearance. The block appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. It is suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as in landscaping, retaining walls, or as a decorative boundary.
description: A rectangular, layered stone block with rough, banded surfaces in shades of gray and brown, showing natural weathering and solid construction.
description_view_0: The long side of the block is visible, showcasing prominent horizontal layers and a rough, banded texture.
description_view_1: The short end of the block is shown, revealing the thickness of the layers and a more compact, vertical profile.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, similar to view 0, with clear stratification and a slightly lighter coloration on the upper layers.
description_view_3: The other short end is shown, with some minor chipping and irregularities, and the layered structure is clearly visible.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface weathering with some minor chipping and color variation, indicating exposure to the elements over time.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: typically interlocked or aligned with other blocks in a continuous wall or barrier
